notes: Planning expansion seeds for v4.2.3A. 38 new M3 scenarios across 8 new board families. Authored to fill priority coverage gaps from v4.2.3 baseline (pot_odds_defense, blocker_raise, slowplay_call, protection_raise, domination_fold, bluff_catch, nut_flush_draw). Each scenario carries a uniquenessNote explaining the new strategic dimension it adds. Migration to production via tools/migrate-expansion-v4.2.3A.ps1.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Ah 9c on

explanation.short: Top two on A-high two-tone -- raise for value AND protection from FDs.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets A-high two-tone wide; calling range contains many A-x and FD bluffs that pay off.
explanation.defenseLogic: Top two is far ahead of villain's range; raising builds the pot before turns kill action.
explanation.handLogic: A9 makes top two pair (As + 9s blocked). Worse hands: Ax weaker kickers, 99/44 sets are rare; FD calls a small raise.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise charges A-x and FDs; big raise also defensible vs FD-heavy range to deny equity.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying top two on a TWO-TONE board lets the FD complete cheaply on the turn.
explanation.takeaway: Top two on two-tone = raise (small or big), not call.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Top two pair lesson on A-high TWO-TONE (vs existing top-set lesson on As8d3h rainbow). Two-tone adds protection-from-FD dimension absent on rainbow.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Ad Qd on

explanation.short: TPGK + backdoor diamond on A-high two-tone -- call to keep BTN bluffs in.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN's c-bet range contains many bluffs and worse Ax; calling preserves value from both.
explanation.defenseLogic: AQ with backdoor diamond comfortably defends; raising folds out air without thin-value gain.
explanation.handLogic: AdQd has top pair + Q kicker + backdoor diamond runner-runner.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise possible vs over-bluffy villains, but call is the higher-EV default OOP.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising TPGK OOP isolates worse made hands and folds out the bluff bucket.
explanation.takeaway: TPGK + BDFD on A-high two-tone = call default, raise vs over-bluffy villains.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: TPGK call lesson with BACKDOOR FD on two-tone (vs existing mid-pair calls on rainbow). Backdoor diamond gives runner-runner equity that anchors the call.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 9h 8h on

explanation.short: Mid pair (9) on A-high two-tone -- call; equity barely above defense threshold.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bet range here is wide with A-x and FD bluffs; mid pair beats most of the bluff bucket.
explanation.defenseLogic: Pair of 9s clears the ~25% defense threshold by a thin margin; raise folds out bluffs and isolates Ax.
explanation.handLogic: 98o has middle pair (9) with no flush draw, no straight draw, just 5 outs to two-pair/trips.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes equity at minimum cost; raising or folding both lose EV.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding mid pair to a small c-bet on A-high over-folds vs villain's wide stab range.
explanation.takeaway: Mid pair without backup on A-high two-tone = call (don't fold, don't raise).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Pot-odds defense lesson -- mid pair NO backdoor on two-tone (existing Th8h on rainbow As8d3h has BDFD). Tests whether hero understands defense threshold without backup equity.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Js Ts on

explanation.short: Suited Broadway FD + 2 overcards on A-high two-tone -- call to realize equity.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets A-high two-tone with many A-x value; check-raising as semi-bluff bloats vs Ax.
explanation.defenseLogic: 9 outs to flush + 6 overcard outs = strong combo equity well above threshold.
explanation.handLogic: JsTs has spade FD + J/T overcard outs on As9s4d.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures equity cheaply; raising forces a tough decision vs Ax that won't fold.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising FD + overcards OOP forces folds from BTN's air bluffs that pay off the call.
explanation.takeaway: Suited Broadway FD on A-high two-tone = call (raise occasionally vs over-folders).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: FD + overcards CALL lesson on A-high two-tone. Distinct from monotone Jh8h4h FD scenarios; here FD + overcard equity must overcome BTN's strong A-x bucket.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Kc Qc on

explanation.short: KQ no-equity on A-high two-tone -- fold; range disadvantage compounds with no draw.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN's c-bet range is A-x heavy with FDs that crush hero.
explanation.defenseLogic: 6 outs to dominated pair on a board where range disadvantage is severe; defense fails.
explanation.handLogic: KcQc has no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw -- just 6 overcard outs that often pair villain's kicker.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action cleanly; calling forces tough turn decisions with no plan.
explanation.commonMistake: "KQ is too pretty to fold" is the most common BB leak on dry A-high.
explanation.takeaway: KQ no equity on dry A-high = automatic fold.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: KQ-no-equity fold on A-high TWO-TONE. Distinct from existing JTo on As8d3h rainbow (different overcard combo, different blocker effect -- KQ blocks AK/KQ value combos in BTN range while JTo blocks none).

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 8h 7h on

explanation.short: Mid pair (8) + backdoor heart on K-high two-tone -- call.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets K-high two-tone wide with K-x value, FD bluffs, and air.
explanation.defenseLogic: Pair of 8s + backdoor heart equity comfortably defends vs small c-bet.
explanation.handLogic: 8h7h has middle pair + BDFD + 2-card backdoor straight (4-5-6-7-8 needs runner-runner).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes equity cheaply; raising bloats OOP vs K-x value that doesn't fold.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding mid pair on K-high two-tone over-folds vs the FD-heavy bluff bucket.
explanation.takeaway: Mid pair + BDFD on K-high two-tone = call.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Mid pair on K-high TWO-TONE with BDFD (vs existing 9d8d on Kh9c4s rainbow). Two-tone adds FD-bluff weight to BTN's c-bet range, raising the EV of calling.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 6s 5s on

explanation.short: Naked low FD on K-high two-tone -- call to realize FD equity.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets K-high two-tone wide; calling preserves FD equity vs the entire range.
explanation.defenseLogic: 9 outs to flush = ~36% raw equity, well above defense threshold.
explanation.handLogic: 6s5s has 9 outs to a low flush + a backdoor straight (3-4-5-6-7 needs 4 + 7).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes FD cheaply; raising is too thin OOP without an overcard or made hand.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising bare low FDs OOP bloats the pot vs K-x that won't fold to a small raise.
explanation.takeaway: Naked low FD on K-high two-tone = call (raise rare without overcard or made hand).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Naked LOW FD without overcards (vs existing FD scenarios that had overcards). Tests pure FD-equity defense without backup pair outs.

scenarios.blockerNote: As blocks BTN's nut FD combos; weakens BTN's spade-FD bluffing range.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with As Qs on

explanation.short: Nut FD + 2 overcards on K-high two-tone -- premium semi-bluff raise.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets K-high two-tone wide; semi-bluff applies fold equity to weak K-x and air.
explanation.defenseLogic: 9 nut FD outs + 6 overcard outs + As blocker = ~50% equity when called and big fold equity.
explanation.handLogic: AsQs has nut FD + 2 overcards + As blocks BTN's nut FD combos.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise applies pressure now; nut blocker reduces villain's value bluff-catchers.
explanation.commonMistake: Just calling with nut FD + overcards OOP wastes premium fold equity vs a wide c-bet.
explanation.takeaway: Nut FD + overcards on K-high two-tone = semi-bluff raise.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: NUT FLUSH DRAW with blocker (first nut_flush_draw drawCategory in M3). Distinct semi-bluff lesson combining max equity + nut blocker -- different from monotone Jh8h4h scenarios because nut FD with overcards on TWO-TONE has more fold equity.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Kc Jh on

explanation.short: TPGK on K-high TWO-TONE -- raise for protection from flush draws.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets K-high two-tone with K-x weaker kickers, FDs, and air; raising charges FDs and gets called by Kx weaker.
explanation.defenseLogic: Top pair good kicker is ahead of BTN's range but vulnerable to FD turns; raising charges draws.
explanation.handLogic: KcJh has top pair K + J kicker; FD turns kill action and let weaker Kx call cheaply.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise charges FDs and extracts thin value from weaker K-x and FD calls.
explanation.commonMistake: Just calling TPGK on a TWO-TONE lets FDs see free turns and pay off thin.
explanation.takeaway: TPGK on K-high two-tone = raise for protection (call only on rainbow textures).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: PROTECTION_RAISE lesson with TPGK on TWO-TONE (vs rainbow K-high where TPGK calls). Tests recognition that suit texture changes raise/call decision for the same hand class.

scenarios.blockerNote: No relevant blockers; Q and J both pair to dominated kickers vs BTN's K-x.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Qd Jh on

explanation.short: QJ no-equity on K-high two-tone -- fold; pair outs are dominated.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bet range is K-x heavy with FD bluffs; QJ has no draw and dominated pair outs.
explanation.defenseLogic: 6 pair outs but Q-pair loses to KQ, J-pair loses to KJ; reverse implied odds confirm fold.
explanation.handLogic: QJ has no pair, no FD, no straight draw on K-8-3 (gutshot to T is too thin); pair outs face KQ/KJ dominance.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling chases dominated outs into a multi-street pot.
explanation.commonMistake: Calling QJ on K-high "because two overcards" bleeds chips to dominated K-x.
explanation.takeaway: Two overcards dominated by villain's K-x range = fold despite seeming "live" outs.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: DOMINATION_FOLD lesson with QJ (vs existing AhQs domination on Kh9c4s). Different overcard combo -- QJ on K-high two-tone has even worse domination because both Q-pair and J-pair lose to top-pair-better-kicker more often.

scenarios.blockerNote: Ks blocks K-high flushes; Js blocks J-x straight combos.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Ks Js on

explanation.short: Combo draw -- OE + FD + 2 overcards (~15 outs) -- premium semi-bluff raise.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets Q-high two-tone wide; combo equity exceeds 50% vs much of BTN''s range.
explanation.defenseLogic: 9 spade outs + 8 straight outs (some overlap) = ~15 unique outs ~ near coin-flip equity.
explanation.handLogic: KsJs has spade FD + open-ender (need A or 9 for straight) + 2 overcards.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise applies pressure now and sets up turn barrels on the right cards.
explanation.commonMistake: Just calling with 15-out combo draws OOP misses premium fold equity.
explanation.takeaway: Combo draws (FD + OE + overcards) on Q-high two-tone = semi-bluff raise.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: COMBO DRAW SEMI-BLUFF on Q-high two-tone (vs existing 9h8h on QhJh6c which had FD+gutshot, ~12 outs). KsJs has FD + OE = ~15 outs; tests max-combo-equity raise lesson.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Qc Td on

explanation.short: Top two on dynamic Q-high two-tone -- raise for value AND protection.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets Q-high two-tone with overpairs, Q-x, J-x with FD, and air; many turns kill action.
explanation.defenseLogic: Top two is far ahead but vulnerable to FD/OE turns; raising builds the pot now.
explanation.handLogic: QcTd makes top two pair (Q + T); only QQ/TT/66 sets and JxXs straights beat it (rare).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise charges FDs/OE draws and gets called by Qx + overpairs; big raise also defensible.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying top two on a wet two-tone lets straight + flush draws complete cheaply.
explanation.takeaway: Top two on dynamic two-tone = raise (small or big) for value + protection.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: Top two value+protection on Q-high TWO-TONE (vs existing top-set QcQd on QhJh6c). Top TWO is more vulnerable than top SET, so the protection layer is even more important.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 9s 8s on

explanation.short: FD + gutshot (no overcard) on Q-high two-tone -- call to realize equity cheaply.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets Q-high two-tone wide; calling preserves equity at minimum cost.
explanation.defenseLogic: 9 spade outs + 4 gutshot outs = strong combo equity; no overcard equity reduces fold-equity value of raising.
explanation.handLogic: 9s8s has spade FD + gutshot to 7 (5-6-7-8-9 straight).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes 13 outs cheaply; raising is too thin without overcards to add fold equity.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising every FD OOP folds out air bluffs that pay off the call.
explanation.takeaway: FD + gutshot WITHOUT overcards on dynamic two-tone = call (raise needs overcard backup).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: FD + gutshot WITHOUT overcards (vs Board 3.1 KsJs which has overcards). Tests recognition that overcard backup matters for raise EV -- without overcards, call is the higher-EV line.

scenarios.blockerNote: Ace blocks AQ value; K blocks KQ value.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Ac Kc on

explanation.short: AK with BDFD + gutshot + overcards on Q-high two-tone -- call (close decision).
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets Q-high two-tone wide; AK has multiple equity sources to defend.
explanation.defenseLogic: Gutshot to J + 6 overcard outs + backdoor club = ~24% equity, just clearing defense threshold.
explanation.handLogic: AcKc has gutshot (J makes broadway) + 6 overcards + backdoor clubs.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes the multi-source equity; folding is also defensible vs strong villains.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-folding AK with backdoor + gutshot under-defends; auto-raising bloats vs Q-x.
explanation.takeaway: AK with multi-source backdoor equity on Q-high = call (close to fold threshold).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: POT-ODDS DEFENSE lesson with AK on dynamic board where equity is borderline. Tests close call/fold decision with multi-source backdoor equity vs simple "fold AK no pair."

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 5h 4d on

explanation.short: Bottom gutshot (need 3 or 7) no FD on Q-high two-tone -- fold; dominated draw.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bet range here is Q-x value + FD bluffs; 5-4 is dominated by both.
explanation.defenseLogic: 4 gutshot outs (~9% equity) is far below defense threshold; no overcards or backdoor to lean on.
explanation.handLogic: 54o has gutshot to 3 (3-4-5-6-7) on Qs Ts 6d -- 4 outs, no other equity.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action cleanly; calling chases dominated outs.
explanation.commonMistake: "Any pair will do" thinking with 5-4 on broadway boards bleeds chips.
explanation.takeaway: Bottom gutshot without backdoor on broadway boards = fold.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: BOTTOM GUTSHOT FOLD on dynamic broadway. Distinct from existing 8c4c on QhJh6c (backdoor only) -- 5-4 has actual gutshot but still fails because of dominated outs and severe range disadvantage.

scenarios.blockerNote: As blocks BTN's nut flush combos AND nut FD bluffs.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with As 6h on

explanation.short: A-blocker + wheel gutshot on monotone -- call leveraging the As blocker.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets monotone polarized (flushes vs air); As blocks all of BTN''s nut flush combos.
explanation.defenseLogic: Wheel gutshot (need 4) + A-overcard + As blocker reduces villain's value, raising defense EV.
explanation.handLogic: As6h has the As blocker + gutshot to 4 (3-4-5-6-7) + 6 outs to top pair (rare to win at SD).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling preserves the blocker's bluff-catching value; raising can fold out air but is variance-heavy.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding the As-blocker on monotone over-folds vs villain's polar bluff range.
explanation.takeaway: A-blocker on monotone with no spade = call (raise occasionally as blocker pressure).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: A-blocker bluff-catch on MONOTONE without spade (first lesson of this kind in M3). Existing monotone Jh8h4h has 9h8c with 1-card FD -- completely different blocker dynamic.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 8s 6s on

explanation.short: Made middle flush + OESD redraw on monotone -- raise BIG for value.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets monotone with worse flushes, big spade combos, and air; raising gets called by worse.
explanation.defenseLogic: Made flush + 4-out OESD redraw to straight flush -- far ahead of villain's value.
explanation.handLogic: 8s6s has middle flush + open-ender to straight flush (need 4 or 9 of spades = straight flush).
explanation.sizingLogic: Big raise (~4x) targets weaker spade flushes that pay off; small raise also acceptable.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying middle flush on monotone misses value vs weaker flush combos that call.
explanation.takeaway: Made flush on monotone OOP = raise (small or big) for value.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: MADE FLUSH value raise on monotone (vs existing Ah Kh nut flush on Jh8h4h). 8s6s is middle flush -- teaches that not every flush is the nuts; sizing differs from nut flush (small/medium more often than big).

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 6h 5h on

explanation.short: Mid pair (5) + gutshot, no spade on monotone -- fold; equity realization too low.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets monotone polarized; pair of 5s loses to all flushes and is dominated by 6x and 7x.
explanation.defenseLogic: Pair + gutshot raw equity ~25% but realized equity OOP on monotone is much lower.
explanation.handLogic: 6h5h has middle pair (5) + gutshot to 4 (need 4 for wheel); no flush blocker or draw.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes the variance; calling forces tough turn decisions vs the polar range.
explanation.commonMistake: Calling every "pair + gutshot" without a spade on monotone over-defends vs polar ranges.
explanation.takeaway: Pair + gutshot WITHOUT spade on monotone = fold (close decision; depends on opponent).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: POT-ODDS DEFENSE FAIL lesson -- pair + gutshot looks playable on raw equity but realized equity OOP on monotone is below threshold. Distinct close-fold lesson with marginal pair without flush blocker.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Kh Qh on

explanation.short: KQ no spade on monotone low -- automatic fold; zero flush equity.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets monotone with flushes and air; without a spade hero has no flush equity at all.
explanation.defenseLogic: Zero flush outs + 6 overcard outs that often lose to higher pair = far below threshold.
explanation.handLogic: KhQh has no spade, no pair, no straight draw on 7s5s3s -- purely overcard equity.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding is automatic; calling with zero flush equity bleeds chips into a polar range.
explanation.commonMistake: Floating big overcards "because they're Broadway" without a spade on monotone is a major leak.
explanation.takeaway: No spade + overcards on monotone = fold (regardless of overcard rank).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: NO-SPADE OVERCARDS FOLD on monotone. Distinct from existing 6c5d on Jh8h4h (different overcard rank class -- KQ are higher overcards but still dominated; tests that overcard rank doesn't rescue a no-equity hand on monotone).

scenarios.blockerNote: As is the nut-flush blocker on monotone spades; folds out BTN's flush draws and bluffs.

question.prompt: Hero check-raises small with As Kh on 7s 5s 3s vs ~33% c-bet. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: A-high check-raise on monotone -- primary reason is the nut-flush blocker.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN's c-bet range on monotone is polarized (made flushes + air); the As blocks villain''s flush combos.
explanation.defenseLogic: The check-raise's EV comes from blocker pressure, not made-hand strength or draw equity.
explanation.handLogic: AsKh has no pair, no draw -- but As removes the nut-flush combos and most FD bluffs from BTN's value range.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise (~3x) leverages the blocker; sizing should not be big since fold equity drops vs polar value.
explanation.commonMistake: Identifying this as semi_bluff_raise misses the mechanism -- the equity is from BLOCKING value, not from outs.
explanation.takeaway: OOP raise with the nut-flush blocker on monotone = blocker_raise (not semi_bluff).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: BLOCKER_RAISE actionReason (FIRST in M3 -- current count is 0). Tests advanced concept that some OOP raises are reason="blocker" not "semi-bluff" -- important distinction for solver-aware play.

scenarios.blockerNote: Holds the case 8 (only one in deck after 8c, 8d); blocks BTN's 8x almost entirely.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 8h 7h on

explanation.short: Trips on paired LOW board -- slowplay because BTN c-bets paired-low wide with air.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets paired low boards heavily with air (overcard hands, overpairs); raising folds the air out.
explanation.defenseLogic: Trips is far ahead of villain's range; calling preserves the bluff bucket for turn/river barrels.
explanation.handLogic: 8h7h has trips + a backdoor heart flush + backdoor straight draw (5-6-7-8-9 needs 5+6 or 9).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling lets BTN barrel air on later streets; raising small folds out almost everything that doesn't have 88.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising trips on paired-low folds out the wide air range and isolates better hands (rare).
explanation.takeaway: Trips on paired LOW = slowplay call; raise only on dynamic boards where protection matters.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: SLOWPLAY_CALL with TRIPS on paired LOW (vs existing AhKh trips on KcKd7s paired HIGH). Different board context -- paired LOW shifts BTN range toward more air vs paired HIGH where BTN has K-x value combos.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 5h 5d on

explanation.short: Pocket fives underpair on 88x -- call to bluff-catch villain's air c-bets.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets paired-low with overcards and air; pocket 5s beats the entire bluff range.
explanation.defenseLogic: Pair beats most of villain's c-bet range; raise folds out the bluffs that pay off the call.
explanation.handLogic: 55 has an underpair to 8 but overpair to 3; only 88, 99-AA, and 8x beat us.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling preserves villain's bluff bucket; raising isolates better hands (overpairs, 8x).
explanation.commonMistake: Folding any pair on paired-low boards over-folds vs villain's wide stab range.
explanation.takeaway: Any small pair on paired-low = call (bluff-catch).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: UNDERPAIR BLUFF-CATCH on paired LOW (vs existing 8d8s on KcKd7s paired HIGH). Different range dynamics -- paired-low BTN range is air-heavier, making the bluff-catch even more profitable.

scenarios.blockerNote: A and K block BTN's top-of-range overpair value (AA, KK).

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Ah Ks on

explanation.short: AK no pair on paired-low -- call as a bluff-catcher; A blocks villain's top value.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets paired-low wide with air; AK with A-blocker beats the entire air range.
explanation.defenseLogic: 6 overcard outs + ace-high showdown beats villain's air; A blocks AA and AK value.
explanation.handLogic: AhKs has no pair on 8c8d3s but A blocks AA top-of-range and 6 overcards can pair best.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain's air bluffs; folding under-defends vs the wide stab range.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-folding AK on paired-low because "no pair" misses the A-blocker bluff-catch line.
explanation.takeaway: AK on paired-low = call (bluff-catch with A-blocker), not fold.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: AK NO-PAIR BLUFF-CATCH on paired-low. Distinct from existing AhKc fold on 8s7d5h connected board -- paired-low is range-stab-heavy texture where AK has bluff-catching equity; connected-low is BB-favored texture where AK should fold. Different board class teaches different defense math.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Tc 9c on

explanation.short: T9 with backdoor club + 2 overcards on paired-low -- call to realize backdoor equity.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets paired-low with mostly air; T9 with 2 overcards + backdoor FD beats much of that range.
explanation.defenseLogic: 6 overcard outs + backdoor club + backdoor straight = ~22% combined equity, just clearing threshold.
explanation.handLogic: Tc9c has 6 overcard pair outs, backdoor club FD, and backdoor straight (5-6-7-8-9 or 6-7-8-9-T).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain's air at minimum cost; folding over-folds; raising bloats vs no fold equity.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding T9 with backdoor on paired-low under-defends vs villain's wide stab.
explanation.takeaway: T9 with multi-source backdoor on paired-low = call (close to fold threshold).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: POT-ODDS DEFENSE with TWO overcards + BDFD on paired-low. Tests recognition that backdoor equity matters at the defense threshold -- distinct from "auto-fold no pair" instinct on paired boards.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Qd Jh on

explanation.short: QJ no equity on paired-low -- fold; even paired boards have a defense floor.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bet range still has overpairs and 8-x; QJ has no draw, no FD, no relevant blocker.
explanation.defenseLogic: 6 pair outs minus dominated by villain's overpairs (KK/QQ blocks J-pair) = below threshold.
explanation.handLogic: QJo on 8c8d3s has no pair, no FD, no straight draw, no relevant blocker.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action; calling chases dominated outs into a polarized range.
explanation.commonMistake: Believing "any two cards defends paired boards" is a frequency leak.
explanation.takeaway: Even on paired low boards, naked overcards without backup = fold.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: PAIRED-LOW FOLD lesson -- proves paired boards still have a defense floor. Distinct from #5.3 (AhKs) by lacking the A-blocker; tests that not every "two overcards" defends.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 9c 9s on

explanation.short: Top set on connected rainbow -- raise for protection from straights and overpairs.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets connected boards with overpairs, top pair, and many straight draws.
explanation.defenseLogic: Top set is huge but vulnerable on a board with 7-T straights coming; protection is essential.
explanation.handLogic: 9c9s makes top set on 9d8c6h; only 88, 66, T7 straights beat us (rare).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise charges OE/gutshots and gets called by overpairs; big raise also defensible.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying top set on connected boards lets straight draws complete cheaply.
explanation.takeaway: Top set on connected boards = raise (small or big) for value AND protection.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: TOP SET PROTECTION on connected rainbow (vs existing 5c5d BOTTOM set on 8s7d5h). Different position on board -- top set is best by amount but more vulnerable to straight completions; lesson tests recognition that even top set needs protection on connected textures.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 8h 6c on

explanation.short: Two pair (8 + 6) + gutshot to 7 on connected rainbow -- raise for value + protection.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets connected with overpairs, top pair, and many draws; two pair is way ahead but vulnerable.
explanation.defenseLogic: Two pair is far ahead now but loses to 7-x straight on the turn; raising charges those draws.
explanation.handLogic: 86o makes top + bottom pair (8 + 6) + gutshot to 7 (5-6-7-8-9 straight).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise gets called by overpairs and 9-x and folds OE/gutshot draws; big also acceptable.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying two pair on a wet connected board folds to a 7 turn most of the time.
explanation.takeaway: Two pair on connected boards = raise for value + protection from completing straights.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: TWO-PAIR with gutshot value-raise on connected board. Distinct from set lessons -- two pair is more vulnerable than set, requiring more aggressive protection.

question.prompt: Hero check-raises small with Tc 7c on 9d 8c 6h vs ~33% c-bet. What is the primary reason?

explanation.short: OE + backdoor club + middle pair on connected -- semi-bluff raise.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets connected wide; T7 with multiple equity sources is a balanced semi-bluff.
explanation.defenseLogic: 8 OE outs + 3 backdoor clubs + middle pair (7) = strong combined equity.
explanation.handLogic: Tc7c has open-ender (need 5 or T for straight) + middle pair + backdoor club FD on 9d8c6h.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise applies fold equity + sets up turn barrels; calling also defensible to realize equity.
explanation.commonMistake: Identifying as protection_raise misses the lesson -- middle pair is too weak to be the value engine.
explanation.takeaway: OE + middle pair + BDFD on connected = semi_bluff_raise (the OE drives the EV).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: SEMI-BLUFF REASON_CHOICE with mid-pair component (vs existing Td9d on 8s7d5h which was OE+overcards). Tests distinguishing semi_bluff_raise from protection_raise when hand has both draw and made-hand components.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Js Th on

explanation.short: JT with OESD + 2 overcards on connected rainbow -- call to realize equity.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets connected wide; JT has multiple equity sources well above defense threshold.
explanation.defenseLogic: 8 OE outs (need 7 or Q) + 6 overcard outs = strong combined equity.
explanation.handLogic: JsTh has open-ender to 7 or Q on 9d8c6h + 2 overcards.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes the equity at minimum cost; raising semi-bluff also defensible.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding JT with OE + overcards on connected over-folds vs villain's wide range.
explanation.takeaway: OE + overcards on connected boards = call (raise as alt vs over-bluffy villains).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: OE + OVERCARDS CALL on connected (vs Board 6.3 reason_choice for raise line). Different from existing 9c8c on 8s7d5h (top pair + gutshot -- has made hand) -- JT is pure-draw + overcards, tests pure equity-realization defense.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Ad Qs on

explanation.short: AQ no draw on connected low rainbow -- fold; range disadvantage with no equity.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets connected with overpairs, top pair, draws -- AQ has no equity to defend.
explanation.defenseLogic: 6 overcard outs that often lose to better pair; no straight or flush equity.
explanation.handLogic: AdQs has no pair, no draw, no relevant blocker on 9d8c6h.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding cleanly avoids variance; calling chases dominated outs.
explanation.commonMistake: Floating AQ "because the cards are big" on connected boards bleeds chips.
explanation.takeaway: Naked AQ on connected low = fold (range disadvantage compounds with no equity).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: AQ NO-EQUITY FOLD on connected (similar lesson to AhKc on 8s7d5h but with different overcard pair AQ vs AK). AQ is even worse because Q-pair more often dominated than K-pair.

scenarios.blockerNote: Holds the case T (only one in deck after Tc, Th); blocks BTN's T-x almost entirely.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Td 9d on

explanation.short: Trips with weak kicker on paired T -- slowplay because BTN's c-bet range is air-heavy.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets paired T wide with overpairs and air; raising folds out the air bluffs.
explanation.defenseLogic: Trips dominates villain's c-bet range; calling preserves villain's bluff bucket for turn/river barrels.
explanation.handLogic: Td9d has trips + a backdoor diamond + backdoor straight (need 7+8 for 6-7-8-9-T).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling lets BTN barrel air on later streets; raising small folds out almost everything.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-raising trips on paired boards isolates better hands and folds out the wide bluff bucket.
explanation.takeaway: Trips on paired board (especially weak kicker) = slowplay call.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: SLOWPLAY_CALL with TRIPS WEAK KICKER on paired T (vs existing AhKh trips top-kicker on KcKd7s). Weak-kicker trips has even more reason to slowplay because raising loses value on the few hands that pay off.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 6c 5c on

explanation.short: Bottom pair (6) + backdoor club on paired T -- bluff-catch villain's air.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets paired T wide with overcards and air; pair of 6s beats the entire bluff range.
explanation.defenseLogic: Pair + BDFD comfortably defends vs the air-heavy stab range.
explanation.handLogic: 6c5c has bottom pair + backdoor club FD + backdoor straight (need 4+7 for 4-5-6-7-8 -- wait that's 6-card; correct: 4-5-6-7-8 only with both 4 and 8 = runner-runner OE).
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures bluffs; raising folds them out and isolates better hands (overpairs, T-x).
explanation.commonMistake: Folding any pair on paired T over-folds vs villain's stab range.
explanation.takeaway: Bottom pair + backdoor on paired T = call (bluff-catch).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: BOTTOM-PAIR BLUFF-CATCH on paired T (distinct from underpair bluff-catches and from mid-pair on Kc Kd 7s). Specifically tests defense with bottom pair, which is a different value class.

scenarios.blockerNote: A and K block BTN's top-of-range overpair value.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Ah Kc on

explanation.short: AK no pair on paired T -- call as a bluff-catcher; A blocks villain's top value.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets paired T wide with air; AK with A-blocker beats the air range.
explanation.defenseLogic: 6 overcard outs + ace-high showdown; A blocks AA/AT and reduces villain's value.
explanation.handLogic: AhKc has no pair on Tc Th 6s but A blocks AA top-of-range and 6 overcards can pair best.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling captures villain's air bluffs; folding under-defends vs the wide stab range.
explanation.commonMistake: Auto-folding AK on paired boards because "no pair" misses the A-blocker bluff-catch line.
explanation.takeaway: AK on paired-T = call (bluff-catch with A-blocker).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: AK BLUFF-CATCH on paired T (distinct from #5.3 AhKs on paired LOW). Different paired board context -- paired T is closer to BTN range parity than paired LOW, making the call slightly tighter.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 8h 8d on

explanation.short: Pocket 8s underpair on paired T -- bluff-catch; raise folds out worse.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets paired T wide with overcards and air; 88 beats the entire bluff range.
explanation.defenseLogic: Underpair to T but overpair to 6; only better overpairs and T-x beat us (rare in BTN c-bet range).
explanation.handLogic: 88 has an underpair to top board card but is well above the air range.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling preserves villain's bluffs; raising isolates overpairs and folds out air.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding small overpairs on paired-T over-folds vs the air-heavy stab range.
explanation.takeaway: Mid pocket pair on paired-T = call (bluff-catch), not raise/fold.

scenarios.uniquenessNote: POCKET-PAIR UNDERPAIR BLUFF-CATCH on paired T (distinct from 5h5d on paired LOW). Different middle pair on different paired board -- tests recognition that paired-board defense extends to underpairs.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 5d 4d on

explanation.short: OESD + BDFD on rag board -- call; equity comfortably above pot-odds threshold.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets rag boards with overpairs and air; 54 has multiple equity sources to defend.
explanation.defenseLogic: 8 OE outs (3-4-5-6-7 needs 7; 4-5-6-7-8 needs 7 and 8 = wait needs 7 only for one straight; or need 5 or A for wheel) + backdoor diamond + 5-overcard.
explanation.handLogic: 5d4d has open-ender (need 5 or A for wheel/8 for top straight) + backdoor diamond.
explanation.sizingLogic: Calling realizes equity cheaply; raising can fold out air but bloats vs overpairs.
explanation.commonMistake: Folding OESD on rag boards over-folds vs villain's wide stab.
explanation.takeaway: OESD + BDFD on rag = call (one of the strongest defense candidates on dry boards).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: POT-ODDS DEFENSE with OESD + BDFD on RAG board (no overcards, just draw equity). Distinct from existing OESD scenarios on connected boards which had overcard backup.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with 6h 6d on

explanation.short: Top set on rag board -- raise small for value; overpairs pay off.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bet range here is overpair-heavy (TT-AA) plus air; overpairs call a small raise.
explanation.defenseLogic: Top set is far ahead of villain's range; sizing should be small to keep overpairs in.
explanation.handLogic: 66 makes top set on 6-3-2; only 33, 22 sets and 5-4 straights beat us (rare).
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise gets called by overpairs and air with showdown; big raise folds out worse.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying top set on rag is acceptable but small raise extracts thin value from overpairs.
explanation.takeaway: Top set on rag = small raise for value (big raise sizing-error vs the overpair-heavy range).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: TOP SET on RAG (vs existing 8c8h top set on As8d3h with A high). Different range interaction -- rag-board BTN range is overpair-heavy, requiring small raise sizing vs A-high where the value targets are A-x.

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Ah As on

explanation.short: AA overpair on rag board -- raise for value; lower overpairs pay off.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets rag boards with overpairs (KK, QQ, JJ, TT, 99) and air; lower overpairs call a small raise.
explanation.defenseLogic: AA dominates villain's c-bet range; raising extracts value from KK-99 that won't fold.
explanation.handLogic: AhAs has overpair (no FD, no draws available); only sets of 6/3/2 and rare 5-4 straights beat us.
explanation.sizingLogic: Small raise charges lower overpairs and gets called by air with showdown; big raise folds out worse.
explanation.commonMistake: Slowplaying AA on rag misses thin value from KK-99 that pay off the small raise.
explanation.takeaway: AA on rag = small raise (do NOT slowplay overpairs on dry boards; thin value matters).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: AA OVERPAIR value-raise on RAG. Distinct from set lessons -- overpair sizing logic differs (smaller because overpair vulnerability is lower than set vulnerability on dynamic boards).

question.prompt: BTN c-bets ~33% pot. What's hero's best action with Ks Qc on

explanation.short: KQ no equity on rag board -- fold; pure overcards realize too little OOP.
explanation.rangeContext: BTN c-bets rag with overpair-heavy range; KQ has no draw, no flush, no relevant blocker.
explanation.defenseLogic: 6 pair outs that often lose to dominating Q-pair (KQ) or K-pair (KK); pot-odds defense fails.
explanation.handLogic: KsQc has no pair, no FD, no straight draw on 6c3d2h.
explanation.sizingLogic: Folding closes action cleanly; calling burns chips bluff-catching with no equity.
explanation.commonMistake: "KQ is too pretty to fold" leaks chips on rag boards where overpairs dominate.
explanation.takeaway: Naked KQ on rag boards = fold (no equity, no realization).

scenarios.uniquenessNote: KQ NO-EQUITY FOLD on RAG (vs Board 1.5 KcQc on As9s4d which is A-high). RAG board has overpair-heavy BTN range vs A-high which has A-x heavy -- different domination vector but same conclusion: fold.
